description: Phase 4: Integrations - HubSpot CRM sync (OAuth2, bi-directional contacts, lead stage push), Calendar booking link generation, Slack notifications (OAuth2, configurable alerts), and Zapier/Webhook marketplace (pre-built templates and recipes).
steps: 1. Add Prisma models: CrmSync (id, integrationId, provider: hubspot/salesforce, direction: inbound/outbound/bidirectional, lastSyncAt, syncStatus, fieldMapping Json, createdAt, updatedAt), CalendarBooking (id, leadId, title, startTime, endTime, meetingLink, status: scheduled/completed/cancelled, bookedAt, createdAt), SlackNotification (id, integrationId, channel, eventTypes String[], isActive, createdAt, updatedAt), WebhookTemplate (id, name, description, category, triggerEvents String[], targetUrl, headers Json?, bodyTemplate String?, isPublic, createdAt, updatedAt), Recipe (id, name, description, category, steps Json, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt).
3. Add shared types: CRM_SYNC_STATUS, CRM_PROVIDER, BOOKING_STATUS, SLACK_EVENT_TYPE, RECIPE_CATEGORY in packages/shared/src/types/index.ts.
4. Add schemas: crmSyncSchema, calendarBookingSchema, slackNotificationSchema, webhookTemplateSchema, recipeSchema in packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts.

5. Create apps/api/src/services/crm-sync.ts: connectCrm(integrationId, authCode), syncContacts(integrationId, direction), pushLeadStage(leadId, stage), pullNewContacts(integrationId), mapFields(lead, fieldMapping), getOAuthUrl(provider), handleOAuthCallback(provider, code).
6. Create apps/api/src/services/calendar-booking.ts: generateBookingLink(leadId, config), processBooking(bookingData), getAvailableSlots(date, timezone), trackBooking(leadId, meetingData).

7. Create apps/api/src/services/slack-notifications.ts: connectSlack(code), sendNotification(integrationId, event, data), configureNotifications(integrationId, config), sendDailyDigest(integrationId).
8. Create apps/api/src/services/webhook-marketplace.ts: listTemplates(category?), createFromTemplate(templateId, config), listRecipes(), activateRecipe(recipeId, config).
9. Create apps/api/src/routes/crm.ts: POST /api/crm/connect, POST /api/crm/sync, GET /api/crm/status, POST /api/crm/oauth/callback, GET /api/crm/field-mapping, PUT /api/crm/field-mapping.
10. Create apps/api/src/routes/calendar.ts: POST /api/calendar/booking-link, GET /api/calendar/bookings, GET /api/calendar/slots, POST /api/calendar/book (public - for leads to book).
11. Create apps/api/src/routes/slack.ts: POST /api/slack/connect, GET /api/slack/channels, PUT /api/slack/notifications, POST /api/slack/test.
13. Register all routes in apps/api/src/index.ts. Calendar booking endpoint for leads is public (no auth).
14. Update mockDb.ts with crmSync, calendarBooking, slackNotification, webhookTemplate, recipe models.
15. Add factory functions: buildCrmSync, buildCalendarBooking, buildSlackNotification, buildWebhookTemplate, buildRecipe.
16. Create service tests: crm-sync.test.ts, calendar-booking.test.ts, slack-notifications.test.ts, webhook-marketplace.test.ts.
17. Create route tests: crm.test.ts, calendar.test.ts, slack.test.ts, marketplace.test.ts.
acceptance_criteria: CRM OAuth2 flow generates correct authorization URL and handles callback
Bi-directional sync maps fields between lead model and CRM contacts
Calendar booking links can be generated and included in templates via {{booking_link}}
Slack connection stores OAuth tokens and sends configurable notifications
Webhook marketplace lists templates and recipes, recipes can be activated
verification: Run `npm test` from /projects/sandbox/lead-genius-pre- and confirm all tests pass
Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in apps/api to confirm no TypeScript errors
